---
title: "UI Storyboard"
---

## App Flow

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
    A["Open the app"] --> B["Choose a module"]
    B --> C["Configure inputs"]
    C --> D["Render outputs"]
    D --> E["Interpret results"]
    E --> F["Download or compare"]

    B --> M1["Time Series Visual Analysis"]
    B --> M2["Time Series Clustering"]
    B --> M3["Forecasting"]
```

## Module 1: Time Series Visual Analysis

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
    A["Select countries or transport series"] --> B["Choose a time window"]
    B --> C["Toggle raw, indexed, or share view"]
    C --> D["Render comparison charts"]
    D --> E["Inspect summary table"]
```

### UI Layout

::: {.storyboard-box}
**Left Panel**

1. Country or mode selector
2. Time window selector
3. View mode toggle
4. Period highlight switch
5. Update button
:::

::: {.storyboard-box}
**Right Panel**

1. Multi-series line chart
2. Indexed comparison chart
3. Share or growth chart
4. Summary table
:::

## Module 2: Time Series Clustering

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
    A["Select a set of country series"] --> B["Set normalization"]
    B --> C["Choose clustering method and k"]
    C --> D["Run clustering"]
    D --> E["Review membership table"]
    D --> F["Review representative patterns"]
    D --> G["Download assignments"]
```

### UI Layout

::: {.storyboard-box}
**Left Panel**

1. Series family selector
2. Country multi-select
3. Time window selector
4. Normalization mode
5. Distance or linkage setting
6. Cluster count slider
7. Run clustering button
8. Insight note describing what to look for
:::

::: {.storyboard-box}
**Right Panel**

1. `Overview` tab: cluster quality strip, insight card, recovery position map, diagnostic table
2. `Patterns` tab: representative pattern plot shown at full readable height
3. `Assignments` tab: membership table, assignment download, and cluster summary table
4. `Series Metrics` tab: recovery metrics table
:::

## Module 3: Forecasting

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
    A["Select a target series"] --> B["Set training cutoff and horizon"]
    B --> C["Choose a forecasting model"]
    C --> D["Run forecast"]
    D --> E["Review forecast plot"]
    D --> F["Review accuracy table"]
    D --> G["Download forecast output"]
```

### UI Layout

::: {.storyboard-box}
**Left Panel**

1. Target series selector
2. Training cutoff input
3. Forecast horizon slider
4. Model selection
5. Confidence interval setting
6. Run forecast button
:::

::: {.storyboard-box}
**Right Panel**

1. Forecast plot
2. Accuracy table
3. Residual diagnostics
4. Forecast download button
:::

## UI Component Mapping

| UI Need | Shiny Component | Reason |
|---|---|---|
| Multi-series comparison | `selectInput` / `pickerInput` | flexible series selection |
| Time window control | `dateRangeInput` | clear start and end selection |
| Raw vs indexed vs share view | `radioButtons` | mutually exclusive display modes |
| Clustering parameter tuning | `sliderInput` | fast constrained control |
| Model execution | `actionButton` | avoid unnecessary reruns |
| Output tables | `DT::datatable` | sortable and filterable |

## Usability Notes

1. Keep the module order aligned with the final website: visual analysis, clustering, forecasting.
2. Keep the labels domain-driven and consistent across Proposal, storyboard, and app.
3. Use the same country names, date windows, and color semantics in all modules.
4. Make the interaction flow simple enough that users can move from exploration to comparison to forecasting without losing context.
